engineGuide.h2: Volkswagen Polo Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volkswagen Polo across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level TDI unit for early Mk4 Polo, non-DPF design simplified maintenance but prone to EGR clogging on urban use.
commonFailures: Injector seal wear causing fuel smell
Turbo actuator sticking on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for AMF Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Updated iteration of AMF with minor emissions improvements, shared failure patterns but slightly improved reliability.
commonFailures: Glow plug relay failure in cold climates
Oil leak from rocker cover gasket
entries.cta: Get quotes for BAY Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel (DPF)
entries.history: DPF-equipped variant for post-2009 Mk5 Polo, improved emissions but added complexity requiring regular regeneration cycles.
commonFailures: DPF regeneration failure on short journeys
EGR valve sticking causing power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for BMS Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Volkswagen's mainstream diesel for Mk5 Polo, balanced power and economy but known for injector and EGR issues at higher mileage.
commonFailures: Piezo injector failure causing rough running
EGR cooler leak leading to coolant loss
Timing chain tensioner wear beyond 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAYC Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Robust pre-DPF diesel from Mk4 era, renowned for longevity but swirl flap issues require attention on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailures: Swirl flap mechanism wear causing rattle
entries.cta: Get quotes for ATL Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel (High Output)
entries.history: Higher-output variant of the 1.9 TDI, popular for performance-oriented owners but shares core failure patterns with ATL.
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear under hard use
entries.cta: Get quotes for ASZ Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: Modern three-cylinder turbo petrol for Mk6 Polo, efficient and refined but direct injection requires periodic carbon cleaning.
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves at 60k+ miles

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for CHYA Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: Slightly higher-output variant of CHYA, same architecture with minor tuning differences for SE and Match trims.
commonFailures: Timing chain guide wear on early units
entries.cta: Get quotes for CHYB Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Volkswagen's high-volume small turbo petrol for Mk5 Polo, widely used but timing chain tensioner design flaw drives significant replacement demand.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling noise
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBZB Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Updated CBZB variant with minor revisions, same core architecture and failure patterns but improved tensioner on later production.
commonFailures: PCV valve failure causing oil leaks

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol (High Output)
entries.history: Higher-output variant of the 1.2 TSI family, offering more power for sportier trims while sharing core design and failure patterns.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CGPA Volkswagen Polo engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: Twincharger (turbo + supercharger) performance unit for upper-spec Mk5 Polo, complex but potent when maintained correctly.
commonFailures: Supercharger clutch wear causing loss of boost
Carbon buildup on direct injection ports

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol Turbo (GTI)
entries.history: Flagship performance engine for Polo GTI, robust K03 turbo design but oil consumption and turbo seal issues drive specialist rebuild demand.
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil seal leak causing smoke
Piston ring wear on high-mileage examples

entries.history: Naturally aspirated three-cylinder entry-level petrol for early Mk4 Polo, simple design but oil consumption issues on worn examples.
commonFailures: Oil consumption from piston ring wear

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Smooth four-cylinder petrol for upper-spec Mk4/Mk5 Polo, reliable when serviced but timing chain guide wear on early units.

commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen Polo Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volkswagen Polo's engine range spans six generations with varying reliability profiles. The 1.2 TSI (CBZB/CBZC) dominates replacement enquiries due to timing chain tensioner failures, while TDI diesels (AMF, CAYC) commonly suffer EGR and injector issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Polo.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Volkswagen Polo 1.2 TSI Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Polo 1.2 TSI S, SE, Match (2009-2017, CBZB & CBZC)
problems.rootCause: Original tensioner design lacks sufficient hydraulic damping, allowing chain slack that causes rattling noise and potential valve damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides and chain before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain jump; prevents engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve inspection, tensioner/chain kit replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable if valves undamaged; risk of hidden damage remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for engines with valve damage or over 90k miles; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: If your Polo 1.2 TSI shows early rattling but runs smoothly, a preventative tensioner replacement at a specialist offers good value. However, if the chain has jumped or valves are damaged, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is more cost-effective than dealer-level repairs. On a Polo worth £2,000-£4,500, replacement extends life 50k+ miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CBZB/CBZC engine prices for your Volkswagen Polo

problems.h4: EGR Valve & Cooler Failure - Why Volkswagen Polo TDI Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Polo 1.4 TDI, 1.6 TDI (2002-2014, AMF, BAY, BMS, CAYC)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation components clog with carbon deposits from urban driving, restricting flow and triggering limp mode or coolant leaks from cracked EGR coolers.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean EGR valve, or fit blanking plate (where legal)
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may trigger warning lights; not suitable for DPF models

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler assembly, gaskets and associated hoses
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses coolant leak risk; does not prevent future carbon buildup

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with EGR system inspection, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage TDI with multiple faults; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: For early EGR symptoms on a well-maintained Polo TDI under 90k miles, cleaning or blanking (where emissions-compliant) can restore performance affordably. If the cooler has leaked coolant into cylinders or the engine shows oil contamination, replacement is safer and often cheaper than extensive repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CAYC/AMF Volkswagen Polo diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Direct Injection Carbon Buildup - Why Volkswagen Polo TSI Engines Lose Performance
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Polo 1.0 TSI, 1.2 TSI, 1.4 TSI (2009-2024, CHYA, CBZB, CAXA)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection design bypasses intake valve washing effect of port injection, allowing carbon deposits to accumulate and restrict airflow, causing rough running and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast carbon from valves, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Effective preventative maintenance; recommended every 60k miles for DI engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Walnut blasting plus injector cleaning, throttle adaptation, software reset
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive approach for noticeable performance loss; restores factory output

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only justified if carbon damage caused valve/piston damage; rare but possible

problems.recommendation: Carbon buildup is a maintenance issue, not a design flaw. Regular walnut blasting every 60k miles prevents performance loss. Only consider replacement if severe deposits caused mechanical damage - uncommon with proper servicing.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CHYA/CBZB Volkswagen Polo petrol engine prices

problems.h4: Injector Failure on TDI Diesels - Why Volkswagen Polo Engines Run Rough
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Polo 1.4 TDI, 1.6 TDI (2009-2014, BMS, CAYC)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injectors on common-rail TDI engines suffer internal wear or seal failure, causing misfires, excessive smoke, and potential cylinder damage from fuel wash.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injector, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for isolated injector failure on otherwise healthy engine

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all injectors, seals, coding and adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses age-related wear across all cylinders; prevents repeat failures

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with injector set included, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage TDI with injector failure plus other wear items

problems.recommendation: If only one injector has failed on a Polo under 100k miles with good service history, single replacement is cost-effective. For engines over 110k miles or with multiple injector codes, full set replacement or a rebuilt engine offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt CAYC/BMS Volkswagen Polo diesel engine deals

problems.h4: Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Wear - Why High-Mileage Volkswagen Polo Engines Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Polo 1.2 3-cyl, 1.2 TSI, 1.6 petrol (2002-2017, BMD, CBZB, CFNA)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear or PCV system failure allows oil to enter combustion chambers, causing blue smoke, fouled spark plugs and eventual catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, rocker cover gasket, external seals
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses external leaks; won't fix internal ring wear causing smoke

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip, ring replacement, honing, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Major labour cost; only viable on low-mileage engines with isolated ring wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded rings, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective solution for smoking engines over 100k miles

problems.recommendation: For Polo petrol engines showing oil consumption over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine is almost always better value than a ring job. The labour cost of an in-situ rebuild approaches replacement cost, without the warranty or upgraded components.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CBZB/CFNA Volkswagen Polo engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen Polo
finalCta.paragraph: The problems above represent the most common failure patterns driving Volkswagen Polo engine replacement enquiries across the UK. Whether you're dealing with a rattling 1.2 TSI, a limp-mode TDI, or a smoking high-mileage petrol, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen Polo Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen Polo and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volkswagen Polo Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volkswagen Polo across UK generations is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes.

items.title: Volkswagen Polo 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4L TDI powered entry and mid-spec Mk4 and early Mk5 Polos, offering strong fuel economy for urban and mixed driving. Non-DPF variants (AMF, BAY) are simpler to maintain, while later DPF-equipped BMS units require regular regeneration cycles to avoid blockages.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration failure on short journeys
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Polo 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Polo 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L TDI replaced the 1.4L in later Mk5 Polos, offering improved refinement and torque for motorway driving. The CAYC code is widespread but known for injector and EGR cooler issues at higher mileage, making rebuilt units with upgraded components a popular choice.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner wear beyond 100k miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Polo 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Polo 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The robust 1.9L TDI powered higher-spec Mk4 Polos before emissions regulations shifted focus to smaller displacements. Renowned for longevity when serviced, but swirl flap mechanisms and turbo oil seals require attention on high-mileage examples.
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Polo 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Polo 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Modern three-cylinder turbo petrol units for Mk6 Polos balance efficiency and refinement. Direct injection design requires periodic carbon cleaning, while early CHYA/CHYB codes may exhibit timing chain guide wear on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Polo 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Polo 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The high-volume 1.2L family spans naturally aspirated three-cylinder (BMD/BME) and turbocharged TSI (CBZB/CBZC) variants. TSI units dominate replacement enquiries due to timing chain tensioner issues, while earlier NA engines suffer oil consumption on worn examples.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Polo 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Polo 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: Naturally aspirated 1.4L units (AUB, BBY) powered mid-spec Mk4 Polos with simple, reliable design. Later turbocharged CAXA variants offered performance but added complexity with twincharger design requiring specialist knowledge for repairs.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Polo 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Polo 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: Smooth four-cylinder 1.6L petrol engines (BTS, CFNA) offered refined performance for upper-spec Mk4 and early Mk5 Polos. Generally reliable but timing chain guide wear on early CFNA units and oil separator failure can cause issues on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Polo 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Polo 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The turbocharged 1.8T powered the Polo GTI flagship, delivering genuine hot hatch performance. Robust K03 turbo design but oil consumption and turbo seal issues drive specialist rebuild demand for performance-oriented owners. Not sure which engine size your Volkswagen Polo needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Volkswagen Polo specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo oil seal leak causing smoke

fuelTypes.h2: Volkswagen Polo Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Volkswagen Polo engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.4 TDI to the latest 1.0 TSI.

items.title: Volkswagen Polo Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel Polo engines dominated UK sales from 2002-2014, offering strong economy for high-mileage drivers but requiring careful maintenance to avoid EGR and DPF-related failures.
items.descriptor: Diesel Polo engines dominated UK sales from 2002-2014, offering strong economy for high-mileage drivers but requiring careful maintenance to avoid EGR and DPF-related failures.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen Polo Diesel Engine Families
families: AMF/BAY - 1.4 TDI non-DPF (Mk4), simple but EGR-prone on urban use
BMS - 1.4 TDI DPF (Mk5), emissions-compliant but regeneration-sensitive
CAYC - 1.6 TDI (Mk5), balanced performance but injector/EGR issues at mileage
ATL/ASZ - 1.9 TDI legacy (Mk4), robust but swirl flap maintenance required
knownFor: Excellent motorway fuel economy (55-70 mpg achievable)
EGR valve clogging on short-journey urban use
DPF regeneration issues on BMS/CAYC without regular motorway runs
High UK replacement demand on 1.4/1.6 TDI variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Polo Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Volkswagen Polo Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Polo engines span simple naturally aspirated units to modern turbocharged TSI variants, with the 1.2 TSI family generating the highest replacement enquiries due to timing chain concerns.
items.descriptor: Petrol Polo engines span simple naturally aspirated units to modern turbocharged TSI variants, with the 1.2 TSI family generating the highest replacement enquiries due to timing chain concerns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen Polo Petrol Engine Families
families: BMD/BME - 1.2L 3-cyl NA (Mk4), simple but oil consumption on worn examples
CBZB/CBZC - 1.2 TSI (Mk5/Mk6), high-volume but timing chain tensioner risk
CHYA/CHYB - 1.0 TSI (Mk6), efficient but direct injection carbon buildup
CFNA - 1.6L NA (Mk4/Mk5), refined but timing chain guide wear on early units
BJX - 1.8T GTI (Mk5), performance-focused with turbo maintenance needs
foundIn: Mk4 (2002-2009) - BMD, BME, AUB, BBY, BTS, ATL, ASZ
Mk5 (2009-2014) - CBZB, CBZC, CAXA, CFNA, BJX
knownFor: 1.2 TSI timing chain tensioner failures driving urgent replacements
Direct injection carbon buildup requiring periodic walnut blasting
Strong reliability on well-serviced NA units (AUB, BBY, BTS)
GTI BJX turbo maintenance critical for longevity

items.title: Volkswagen Polo Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: Later Mk6 Polos introduced mild-hybrid (eTSI) technology for improved efficiency, though pure electric variants were not offered in the UK Polo range.
items.descriptor: Later Mk6 Polos introduced mild-hybrid (eTSI) technology for improved efficiency, though pure electric variants were not offered in the UK Polo range.
families: 48-volt starter-generator recovers braking energy for electric assist
Enables coasting with engine off and smoother start/stop operation

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen Polo Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Volkswagen Polo's production timeline from 2002 to present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Mk4 Launch: Entry Petrol & Robust TDI
years.description: The Mk4 Polo launched with simple naturally aspirated petrol engines and Volkswagen's renowned 1.9 TDI diesel, establishing the model's reputation for practicality.
keyChanges: New PQ24 platform replacing previous generation
Introduction of 1.2L 3-cylinder petrol (BMD/BME) for entry trims
1.4L 16v petrol (AUB/BBY) and 1.6L (BTS) for mid/upper trims
1.4 TDI (AMF/BAY) and 1.9 TDI (ATL/ASZ) diesel options
knownFor: Simple, easy-to-maintain engine designs
1.9 TDI renowned for longevity with proper servicing
Early 1.2L 3-cyl can suffer oil consumption on high mileage
Swirl flap issues on 1.9 TDI require periodic inspection
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2005 Volkswagen Polo replacement engines
years.ticker: Polo 1.2 oil consumption • Polo 1.9 TDI swirl flap • Polo 1.4 petrol timing belt

years.preview: Mk4 Facelift: Refined Engines & Emissions Focus
years.description: Mid-life updates brought minor engine refinements and early emissions technology, preparing the Polo for upcoming regulations.
keyChanges: Minor power tweaks to 1.4L and 1.6L petrol units
Introduction of DPF on select 1.9 TDI variants for urban compliance
Updated ECU mapping for improved driveability
Interior and exterior styling refresh
knownFor: BlueMotion variants achieve exceptional fuel economy
DPF-equipped diesels require regular motorway runs
CFNA 1.6L generally reliable but watch timing chain guides
Good availability of used engines for this era
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2008 Volkswagen Polo replacement engines

years.preview: Mk5 Launch: TSI Turbo Petrol Arrives
years.description: The all-new Mk5 Polo introduced Volkswagen's small turbo petrol engines, transforming performance and efficiency but bringing new failure patterns.
keyChanges: New PQ25 platform with improved safety and refinement
Introduction of 1.2 TSI turbo petrol (CBZB/CBZC) replacing NA 1.2/1.4
1.6 TDI (CAYC) replaces 1.9 TDI for mainstream diesel demand
Start/stop technology on select variants
knownFor: 1.2 TSI timing chain tensioner issues emerge as key failure pattern
CAYC 1.6 TDI offers strong economy but injector/EGR concerns at mileage
GTI BJX delivers genuine hot hatch performance
Start/stop systems require battery health monitoring
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2011 Volkswagen Polo replacement engines
years.ticker: Polo 1.2 TSI rattling noise • Polo CAYC injector failure • Polo GTI turbo maintenance

years.preview: Mk5 Matures: DPF Standard & TSI Refinements
years.description: Mid-cycle updates saw DPF become standard on diesels and minor revisions to TSI tensioners, though failure patterns remained relevant for replacement buyers.
keyChanges: DPF fitted to all new diesel Polos for Euro 5 compliance
Revised tensioner design on later 1.2 TSI production (post-2013)
Introduction of Beats Edition and R-Line trim levels
Updated infotainment and driver assistance features
knownFor: Later CBZC units show improved tensioner reliability but risk remains
DPF maintenance critical for urban-driven TDI Polos
Beats Edition popular but engine compatibility same as standard trims
Strong used engine availability for this high-volume generation
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2014 Volkswagen Polo replacement engines
years.ticker: Polo DPF warning light • Polo 1.2 TSI chain rattle • Polo BlueMotion economy

years.preview: Mk6 Launch: Downsized Turbo Petrol Dominates
years.description: The Mk6 Polo embraced engine downsizing, with 1.0 TSI replacing larger petrol units and refined TDI diesels continuing for high-mileage buyers.
keyChanges: New MQB A0 platform with improved efficiency and technology
Introduction of 1.0 TSI three-cylinder turbo (CHYA/CHYB)
1.2 TSI continued alongside new 1.0 for transition period
Enhanced driver aids and connectivity features
knownFor: 1.0 TSI efficient but direct injection carbon buildup requires attention
Carryover CAYC/BMS engines maintain known failure patterns
Improved refinement and NVH across the range
Strong demand for rebuilt 1.0 TSI as first high-mileage examples emerge
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2017 Volkswagen Polo replacement engines
years.ticker: Polo 1.0 TSI carbon cleaning • Polo 1.2 TSI replacement • Polo diesel EGR

years.description: Mid-life updates brought styling refreshes, enhanced tech and the first mild-hybrid options, though core engine architectures remained consistent.
keyChanges: Exterior and interior styling refresh
Introduction of 1.0 eTSI mild-hybrid variants (2020)
Updated infotainment with smartphone integration
Enhanced driver assistance systems
mainEngines: CAYC - 1.6 TDI diesel 90hp (SE, Match - limited availability)
knownFor: 1.0 TSI remains high-volume with carbon buildup maintenance needs
Mild-hybrid systems require specialist knowledge for replacement
Strong reliability on well-serviced examples
Growing demand for rebuilt 1.0 TSI as warranty-expired examples age
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2020 Volkswagen Polo replacement engines

years.description: Latest Polos feature refined 1.0 TSI engines with mild-hybrid technology, though replacement demand remains focused on earlier high-mileage examples.
keyChanges: Mild-hybrid (eTSI) becomes mainstream option
Enhanced emissions compliance and efficiency tuning
Advanced driver assistance and connectivity as standard
Continued focus on 1.0 TSI as sole petrol option for most trims
mainEngines: CHYA/CHYB eTSI - 1.0L mild hybrid 95-110hp (SE, Match, R-Line)
knownFor: Early reliability data positive but long-term patterns still emerging
Mild-hybrid complexity requires specialist suppliers for replacements
Strong residual values keep replacement demand moderate for now
Focus shifts to maintenance and carbon cleaning for longevity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2021-present Volkswagen Polo replacement engines

faq.h2: Volkswagen Polo Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volkswagen Polo engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A rebuilt Volkswagen Polo engine typically costs £650-£2,250 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £450-£850 labour.
keyPoints: 1.4 TDI rebuilt: £650-£1,400 supply only
1.2 TSI rebuilt: £750-£2,100 supply only
Used engines from £345 but shorter warranty
Always compare multiple quotes for best value
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Polo engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit my Volkswagen Polo?
items.answer: Enter your registration above to instantly verify your exact Volkswagen Polo engine code. Common codes include CBZB, CAYC, AMF, CHYA.
keyPoints: DVLA lookup confirms code, fuel type and variant
CBZB/CBZC for 1.2 TSI (2009-2017)
CAYC for 1.6 TDI (2009-2014)
CHYA/CHYB for 1.0 TSI (2014-present)
items.cta: Check your Volkswagen Polo engine code compatibility

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Volkswagen Polo engine?
items.answer: Yes, if your Polo's chassis and body are sound. A rebuilt engine extends life 50k+ miles at a fraction of replacement car cost.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine: £650-£2,250 vs £5k+ for similar used Polo
12-month warranty provides peace of mind
Ideal for SE, Match trims with good service history
Less viable for high-mileage cars with other faults
items.cta: Get a Volkswagen Polo engine replacement quote today

items.question: How long does a Polo engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 48 hours. Full supply & fit takes 1-3 days depending on workshop availability.
keyPoints: Used engines often available next day
Rebuilt units: 2-4 day average lead time
Supply & fit: book 3-5 days ahead
Urgent options available for critical failures
items.cta: Find fast Volkswagen Polo engine supply & fit deals

items.question: What warranty comes with a Polo replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/reconditioned: 12-month standard
Used engines: typically 3-6 months
Warranty covers parts and labour at supplying workshop
Always confirm terms before ordering
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Polo engine warranty options

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Polo?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but require ECU coding, wiring adaptation and specialist knowledge. Stick to original codes for simplest replacement.
keyPoints: CBZB to CBZC generally compatible (same family)
Diesel to petrol swaps complex and often not cost-effective
Always verify compatibility with specialist before ordering
Registration and insurance implications apply
items.cta: Speak to a Volkswagen Polo engine specialist about compatibility

items.question: Are Polo diesel engines more reliable than petrol?
items.answer: Both have strengths: TDI diesels excel at high mileage but need EGR/DPF care; TSI petrols are refined but require carbon cleaning.
keyPoints: 1.9 TDI (ATL/ASZ) renowned for longevity
1.2 TSI (CBZB) timing chain issues well-documented
1.0 TSI (CHYA) efficient but direct injection maintenance needed
Service history matters more than fuel type
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Polo diesel vs petrol engine prices

items.question: How do I know if my Polo needs a new engine?
items.answer: Warning signs include persistent knocking, excessive smoke, loss of power, or warning lights that persist after basic diagnostics.
keyPoints: Rattling on cold start: possible timing chain issue (1.2 TSI)
Blue smoke: oil burning, possible ring wear
Limp mode with EGR/DPF codes: diesel emissions fault
Always get professional diagnosis before committing
items.cta: Get a diagnostic quote for your Volkswagen Polo engine

items.question: Do you offer mobile engine fitting for Polo?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists offering mobile or workshop supply & fit for Volkswagen Polo engine replacements nationwide.
keyPoints: 95% UK postcode coverage for fitting services
Mobile fitting ideal for non-runners or remote locations
Workshop fitting often faster and more cost-effective
All fitters vetted and backed by EngineMarket warranty
items.cta: Book Volkswagen Polo engine supply & fit near you

items.question: Can I get a Polo engine next day delivery?
items.answer: Used and some rebuilt Volkswagen Polo engines can dispatch next day if ordered before 2pm, subject to stock and location.
keyPoints: Used engines: often available from UK breakers immediately
Rebuilt units: confirm lead time with supplier before ordering
Express delivery options available at extra cost
Always verify engine condition and warranty before accepting
